digest.component_or_area: completion-verifier rubric prompt and eval scorers
propositions: A completion-verifier FAIL verdict surfaces unmet criteria piecemeal across rounds, forcing extra remediation cycles instead of enumerating all unmet criteria in one pass.
The completion_verifier.md prompt must require per-criterion exhaustive evaluation, emitting one finding per unmet criterion on FAIL and declaring a subset-only verdict incomplete.

A new deterministic scorer enumerates_all_unmet_criteria is implemented and registered in REGISTRY, scoring 1.0 only when each expected_unmet_criteria substring bijectively matches a distinct finding's criterion field.
The eval spec references the scorer and adds a CV-two-unmet-criteria case with two independent AC gaps, proven offline by validate_eval_spec(strict=True) passing.
A FakeRunner unit test asserts a two-finding FAIL verdict survives reconcile_verdict and the close path with all findings intact.
reconcile_verdict and close-path logic remain byte-unchanged; this is a prompt- and eval-level change only.
